title: Chrysojasminum fruticans
taxonomy: Chrysojasminum fruticans is a species of flowering plant in the family Oleaceae. It is native to the Mediterranean region and is commonly known as the 'yellow jasmine'. The plant is an evergreen shrub that can grow up to 3 meters in height. Chrysojasminum fruticans is closely related to other flowering plants in the Oleaceae family, such as forsythia and lilac. The classification of Chrysojasminum fruticans is further divided into two main subspecies.

intro: Chrysojasminum fruticans, also known as yellow jasmine or yellow jessamine, is a flowering vine native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its bright yellow, star-shaped flowers. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia.
